title: Reading and Use of English - Part 7
description: You are going to read an article about four people discussing what they have learned from family relationships. For questions 43–52, choose from the people (A–D). The people may be chosen more than once.

texts.C:
### Emily, 28, Journalist
Growing up in a big family taught me patience. [43]With so many siblings, I had to learn to share and compromise[/43]. My parents encouraged us to talk about our feelings, which [47]helped me become a good listener[/47]. Now, I try to [50]keep in touch with everyone, even though we live far apart[/50].
texts.B:
### David, 40, Engineer
[44]My parents divorced when I was young, so I spent time in two very different households[/44]. This experience made me adaptable and open-minded. [48]I learned to see things from different perspectives[/48]. [51]Now, I try to create a stable environment for my own children[/51].
texts.D:
### Zoe, 23, Student
[45]My grandmother played a big role in raising me[/45]. She taught me the importance of kindness and respect. [49]I often turn to her for advice when I face challenges[/49]. [52]Family, for me, means supporting each other no matter what[/52].
texts.A:
### Mark, 35, Chef
I’m an only child, so [46]I spent a lot of time with adults growing up[/46]. This made me independent, but [47]sometimes I wished for siblings to share things with[/47]. [49]I’ve learned to value close friendships as much as family[/49].

questions.question: learned to compromise because of their family situation?
questions.explanation: Emily had to share and compromise with many siblings.

questions.question: grew up experiencing two different family environments?
questions.explanation: David spent time in two households after his parents divorced.

questions.question: was mainly raised by a grandparent?
questions.explanation: Zoe’s grandmother played a big role in raising her.

questions.question: spent much of their childhood with adults?
questions.explanation: Mark, as an only child, spent a lot of time with adults.

questions.question: became a good listener thanks to family discussions?
questions.explanation: Emily became a good listener from family conversations.

questions.question: learned to appreciate different viewpoints?
questions.explanation: David learned to see things from different perspectives.

questions.question: relies on a family member for advice in difficult times?
questions.explanation: Zoe turns to her grandmother for advice.

questions.question: makes an effort to stay connected with family despite distance?
questions.explanation: Emily keeps in touch with family even though they live far apart.

questions.question: aims to provide stability for their own children?
questions.explanation: David tries to create a stable environment for his children.

questions.question: believes family means unconditional support?
questions.explanation: Zoe says family means supporting each other no matter what.
